text.title: Responsive Display Ads: Asset Requirements and Image Upload
text.summary: Mandatory and optional asset counts for responsive display ads, image upload pattern, and performance report query.

rule.statement: Provide the maximum asset count Google allows: 5 headlines, 5 descriptions, 15 marketing images, 5 logos. More assets give the algorithm more combinations to test, improving performance over time.
rule.rationale: Google automatically tests asset combinations. Providing only the minimum (1 headline, 1 description, 1 image) eliminates the optimization surface and treats a responsive ad like a static ad.

section.title: Image Upload
section.p: Images must be uploaded as Asset objects before being referenced in a responsive display ad. The asset_service.mutate_assets() call returns a resource_name used in the AdImageAsset reference.

rule.statement: Upload images as binary content (requests.get(image_url).content), not as URLs. The API does not accept external image URLs directly in the asset payload.
rule.rationale: The Google Ads API requires binary image data in the asset creation request. Passing a URL causes a validation error without a clear error message.

section.reference: Asset dict structure for responsive display ad creation

section.title: Performance Reporting
section.p: Query detail_placement_view for placement-level performance. Filter by campaign.id and date range. Sort by metrics.impressions DESC to identify top and bottom-performing placements.
